episode.title: Le jeu de cartes 🎧
episode.source: Real Life French — Choses à Savoir

transcript.speaker: Julien
transcript.fr: Tiens, j’ai découvert ce nouveau jeu de cartes sur internet !
transcript.en: I've found this new card game online!

transcript.speaker: Louise
transcript.fr: C’est vrai que c’est cool ce type de jeu, car ça se transporte partout, et c’est toujours l’occasion de bien se marrer !
transcript.en: It's true that this type of game is cool, because you can take it anywhere and it's always an opportunity to have fun!

transcript.speaker: Julien
transcript.fr: Ouais, notamment quand tu prends l’apéro avec des potes !
transcript.en: Yeah, especially when you're having a drink with friends!

transcript.speaker: Julien
transcript.fr: D’ailleurs, c’est un bon prétexte pour boire un coup, non ?
transcript.en: Besides, it's a good excuse to have a drink, right?

transcript.speaker: Louise
transcript.fr: Oui, mais te plains pas si tu perds car t’es déconcentré par les chips et la boisson !
transcript.en: Yes, but don't complain if you lose because you're distracted by the chips and drink!

cards.fr: J’ai découvert ce nouveau jeu de cartes sur internet !
cards.en: I've found this new card game online!
cards.note: Use 'découvrir' to talk about finding or discovering something new.

cards.fr: C’est cool ce type de jeu, car ça se transporte partout !
cards.en: This type of game is cool because you can take it anywhere!
cards.note: Use 'se transporter' to describe something that can be easily moved or taken somewhere.

cards.fr: C’est toujours l’occasion de bien se marrer !
cards.en: It's always an opportunity to have fun!
cards.note: Use 'se marrer' to describe having fun or enjoying oneself.

cards.fr: Je prends l’apéro avec des potes !
cards.en: I'm having a drink with friends!
cards.note: Use 'prendre l'apéro' to describe having a casual drink with others, often as a social activity.

cards.fr: C’est un bon prétexte pour boire un coup !
cards.en: It's a good excuse to have a drink!
cards.note: Use 'prétexte' to describe a reason or excuse for doing something, often in a casual or social context.

cards.fr: Ne te plains pas si tu perds !
cards.note: Use the imperative 'ne...pas' to give advice or instructions, and 'se plaindre' to describe complaining.

cards.fr: Je suis déconcentré par les chips et la boisson !
cards.en: I'm distracted by the chips and drink!
cards.note: Use 'déconcentré' to describe being distracted or unable to focus due to something.
